# Prompt [NUMBER] · [Prompt Title]

**Domain:** [Domain Name]
**Level:** Essential / Practitioner / Advanced
**Use Case:** One sentence. What this produces and why someone would use it.

---

## Variables

Replace all `[BRACKETED]` variables with your specific context before running.

| Variable | What to Enter | Example |
|----------|---------------|---------|
| `[VARIABLE_1]` | Description of what to enter | e.g. concrete example |
| `[VARIABLE_2]` | Description of what to enter | e.g. concrete example |
| `[VARIABLE_3]` | Description of what to enter | e.g. concrete example |

---

## The Prompt

> Copy everything below this line, replace all [VARIABLES], and paste into any LLM.

---

```
<role>
You are a [specific expert role] with [specific experience and domain expertise].
</role>

<context>
[Variable label]: [VARIABLE_1]
[Variable label]: [VARIABLE_2]
[Variable label]: [VARIABLE_3]
</context>

<instructions>
Step 1: [First step — what to analyse or evaluate]

Step 2: [Second step — what to build or calculate]

Step N: Compile everything into the output format specified below.
</instructions>

<output_format>
[EXACT specification of the deliverable structure]

[Define sections, tables, columns, scoring scales]
[Be so specific that there is zero ambiguity about what the output looks like]
</output_format>

<constraints>
- [What to avoid]
- [Quality guardrails]
- [Tone and specificity requirements]
- No em dashes. No filler phrases. Write in British English.
</constraints>

<evaluation_criteria>
Before delivering your output, verify:
1. [Completeness check]
2. [Accuracy check]
3. [Specificity check — no generic or placeholder content]
4. [Format compliance check]
5. [Actionability check — could someone act on this immediately?]
</evaluation_criteria>
```

---

## Why This Works

[One paragraph explaining the engineering decisions behind this prompt — why the structure produces better output than alternatives.]

---

## Sample Output

[One concrete example row, section, or table showing what good looks like.]
